message:
fix(packs/core/git): catch combined push force flags, avoid branch-name false-match

Two issues in the push-force-short rule:

1. Combined short-flag forms (-uf, -fv, -vf, -fuv) evaluate to `-f` at
   parse time but weren't matched by `.*-f\b`. All of these overwrite
   remote history.

2. `.*-f\b` also false-matched branch names containing `-f`:
     git push origin feature-f    -> false-matched push-force-short
     git push origin hotfix-f     -> false-matched push-force-short

Fixed by switching to the token-walker form
`push\s+(?:\S+\s+)*-[a-zA-Z]*f[a-zA-Z]*\b`. `\S+\s+` steps through
space-separated args one at a time, so hyphens INSIDE a single token
(branch name) are not reachable as flag starts. The flag character
class `[a-zA-Z]*f[a-zA-Z]*` catches every combined form containing `f`.

`--force-with-lease` (safer alternative) continues to be excluded via
the existing negative lookahead on push-force-long (which wins by
being defined first).

Regression tests added for combined forms, branch-name false matches,
and the --force-with-lease carve-out.
